BindingIdParams.description: Request payload for binding-scoped auth methods.
BindingIdParams.title: BindingIdParams

PeerMeta.description:
Supplementary metadata attached to a peer for discovery.

The peer's routing name lives in `TrustedPeer.name` / `comms_name`.
`PeerMeta` carries additional context: what the agent does and
arbitrary key/value labels.

# Serde

Both fields use `skip_serializing_if`, so `PeerMeta::default()`
serializes to `{}`.

description.description: What this agent does (e.g. "Reviews pull requests for style issues").

labels.description:
Arbitrary key/value labels (e.g. `{"lang": "rust", "team": "infra"}`).

`BTreeMap` keeps keys sorted for deterministic serialization.

keep_alive.description: None = inherit persisted session intent, Some(true) = enable, Some(false) = disable.

BindingId.description: Opaque slug identifying a binding inside a realm.

ProfileId.description: Opaque slug identifying an auth profile override on a connection.

WireConnectionRef.description:
Wire projection of [`meerkat_core::ConnectionRef`].

Pure structural shape — no `"realm:binding"` string form. Wave-b deleted
`parse` and `Display` on both the core type and the wire projection so
the colon-joined form cannot travel across wire boundaries.

connection_ref.description:
Phase 4c — realm-scoped binding reference. When set, the session
is built through the realm connection set; when omitted, the
legacy flat `provider + api_key` path is used.

shell_env.description: Per-agent environment variables injected into shell tool subprocesses.

WireMemberRef.description:
Server-resolved opaque handle for a mob member.

Encodes `{mob_id, agent_identity}` as a single base64url-encoded token
that callers treat as opaque. The server resolves the current
`AgentRuntimeId` and fence token against the live mob roster on every
dispatch — clients never reason about `generation` or `fence_token`
directly.

Use [`WireMemberRef::encode`] to produce a token and
[`WireMemberRef::decode`] inside an RPC handler to recover the
`(mob_id, agent_identity)` pair before resolving against the runtime.

MobDefinitionInput.description:
Public mob definition input for `mob/create`.

This mirrors the public creation contract shape. Runtime-owned lifecycle and
bookkeeping fields such as internal owner/runtime bindings,
`session_cleanup_policy`, `is_implicit`, and internal-only profile tool
bundles are intentionally not part of this schema.

Not `Eq`: `profiles` transitively carries float provider params.

MobProfileBindingInput.description:
Profile binding input: either an inline profile or a realm profile reference.

Not `Eq`: `Inline(MobProfileInput)` transitively carries float provider
params (`temperature`, `top_p`) so `Eq` cannot be derived without
losing fidelity.

provider_params.description:
Non-`Eq` field: `WireProviderParamsOverride` contains float scalars
(`temperature`, `top_p`) so the struct can't derive `Eq` without
losing fidelity.

OutputSchema.description:
Configuration for structured output extraction.

When provided to an agent, the agent will perform an extraction turn after
completing the agentic work, forcing the LLM to output validated JSON that
conforms to the provided schema. The extraction JSON becomes the final
response text (schema-only) in [`RunResult`].

compat.description: Compatibility mode for provider lowering (lossy or strict)

name.description: Optional name for the schema (used by some providers)

schema.description: The JSON schema that the output must conform to

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict schema validation (provider-specific)

SchemaFormat.description: Schema format versions supported by Meerkat.

StructuredProviderExtension.description:
Typed non-semantic opaque bag for per-turn provider knobs that cannot be
fully typed without blocking a wave boundary. Explicitly marked
non-semantic and RMAT-exempt.

Use of this type is a deliberate boundary marker: content is passed
through without interpretation. Any consumer that needs to interpret the
content must promote the relevant structure into a proper typed variant
in its own wave.

Relocated from `meerkat_contracts::wire::runtime` into core so
`ProviderTag::Unknown { bag }` can name the bag without a cross-crate
cycle (adversarial review flaw 5). `meerkat-contracts` re-exports this
type so the wire path is preserved.

body.description: Opaque body. Non-semantic — never pattern matched across the wire.

key.description: Opaque key identifying the extension within the namespace.

oneOf.description:
Wire projection of `ProviderTag::Unknown { bag }` — the typed
escape hatch for V3 legacy-row provider knobs that don't (yet) map
to a typed variant.

BridgeBootstrapToken.description:
One-time bootstrap proof exchanged between a mob supervisor and a
member runtime on initial bind.

Transparent over the wire (`#[serde(transparent)]` — a bare JSON string),
but carries a redacting `Debug` impl and has no `Display` impl so the
raw secret cannot accidentally land in logs or panic messages. Treat it
like an API key: read `as_str()` only at the comms/transport boundary.

MobMemberSpecWire.description:
Per-member spec for `mob/ensure_member` and the `desired` entries of
`mob/reconcile`.

Mirrors the essential, codegen-friendly fields of
[`meerkat_mob::SpawnMemberSpec`]. Complex sub-types (tool access policy,
budget split, inherited tool filter, override profile) are not on this
wire surface — callers that need that parity should use the non-declarative
`mob/spawn` method.

agent_identity.description: Stable member identity within the mob.

profile.description: Profile name (role) in the mob definition.

WireRuntimeBinding.description:
Runtime binding for spawn requests.

First step toward identity-first mobs. Carries backend-specific binding
details at spawn time. `External` requires typed process identity; callers
do not supply raw comms peer IDs.

identity.description:
Typed Ed25519 signing identity for the external process. The
canonical comms `PeerId` is derived from this key after the wire
boundary, so callers cannot spoof an unrelated raw peer id.

WireTrustedPeerIdentity.description: Typed external peer identity for public mob wiring surfaces.

oneOf.description: Recoverable Ed25519 public key string in `ed25519:<base64>` form.

MobIdParams.description: Shared request payload for mob methods that address a mob by id.
MobIdParams.title: MobIdParams

agent_identity.description: Stable member identity within the mob.

profile.description: Profile name (role) in the mob definition.

WireHandlingMode.description: Public handling mode for mob member delivery.

WireRenderClass.description: Public render class contract for mob member delivery.

WireRenderMetadata.description: Public render metadata contract for mob member delivery.

WireRenderSalience.description: Public render salience contract for mob member delivery.

MobIngressInteractionParams.description:
Request payload for `mob/ingress_interaction`.

This is the ergonomic "ensure an ingress member, then deliver user input"
path. It composes the existing declarative roster and member-send
semantics without introducing a separate thread/project runtime.
MobIngressInteractionParams.title: MobIngressInteractionParams

WireMobLifecycleAction.description:
Typed lifecycle action for `mob/lifecycle`. Replaces the prior
`action: String` discriminator with an exhaustive enum so callers and
handlers reason about lifecycle transitions through the type system
rather than string folklore.

MobMemberFilterWire.description:
Filter for `mob/list_members_matching`. Non-empty / `Some` fields are
combined conjunctively; an empty filter matches every member.

labels.description: Required exact matches on member labels.

MobMemberParams.description: Shared request payload for mob methods that address one member by identity.
MobMemberParams.title: MobMemberParams

WireHandlingMode.description: Public handling mode for mob member delivery.

WireRenderClass.description: Public render class contract for mob member delivery.

WireRenderMetadata.description: Public render metadata contract for mob member delivery.

WireRenderSalience.description: Public render salience contract for mob member delivery.

MobMemberSendParams.description: Request payload for host-side mob member delivery.
MobMemberSendParams.title: MobMemberSendParams

losing fidelity.

retire_stale.description:
When `true`, members on the roster whose identity is not in the
`desired` set are retired.

MobSpawnParams.description:
Request payload for `mob/spawn`.

The public catalog uses wire-owned projections for stable fields and JSON
extension slots for advanced Rust-native spawn options whose schemas are
not yet owned by `meerkat-contracts`.
MobSpawnParams.title: MobSpawnParams

WireMemberRef.description:
Server-resolved opaque handle for a mob member.

Encodes `{mob_id, agent_identity}` as a single base64url-encoded token
that callers treat as opaque. The server resolves the current
`AgentRuntimeId` and fence token against the live mob roster on every
dispatch — clients never reason about `generation` or `fence_token`
directly.

Use [`WireMemberRef::encode`] to produce a token and
[`WireMemberRef::decode`] inside an RPC handler to recover the
`(mob_id, agent_identity)` pair before resolving against the runtime.

WireWorkOrigin.description:
Origin for `MobSubmitWorkParams`. Replaces the prior free-form
`origin: Option<String>` shape.

MobSubmitWorkParams.description:
Request payload for `mob/submit_work`.

Identifies the member through the opaque [`WireMemberRef`] handle the
server resolves against the live roster — callers do not pass
`generation` or `fence_token`.

work_ref.description:
Optional caller-supplied work reference. When absent the server
generates a fresh UUID.

MobSubmitWorkParams.title: MobSubmitWorkParams

MobPeerTarget.description: Target for a mob wire/unwire call.

WireTrustedPeerIdentity.description: Typed external peer identity for public mob wiring surfaces.

oneOf.description: Recoverable Ed25519 public key string in `ed25519:<base64>` form.

WireTrustedPeerSpec.description:
Minimal trusted peer spec for public mob wiring surfaces.

`identity` is required and resolves to the Ed25519 signing public key
plus the canonical comms `PeerId` derived from that key. MCP callers do
not provide raw peer IDs, and missing key material fails at the boundary.

MobWaitParams.description: Shared request payload for mob readiness waits.
MobWaitParams.title: MobWaitParams

MobPeerTarget.description: Target for a mob wire/unwire call.

WireTrustedPeerIdentity.description: Typed external peer identity for public mob wiring surfaces.

oneOf.description: Recoverable Ed25519 public key string in `ed25519:<base64>` form.

WireTrustedPeerSpec.description:
Minimal trusted peer spec for public mob wiring surfaces.

`identity` is required and resolves to the Ed25519 signing public key
plus the canonical comms `PeerId` derived from that key. MCP callers do
not provide raw peer IDs, and missing key material fails at the boundary.

access_token.description: Access token acquired from a prior Console-OAuth flow.

RealtimeChannelTarget.description:
Target for a public realtime channel.

Two variants, one for each addressing mode:

- `SessionTarget` — standalone sessions (no mob-member continuity). The
  session id is pinned for the channel's lifetime; when that session
  ends, the channel ends.

- `MobMember` — mob-member continuity (W3-H / dogma #4). Identity is the
  canonical anchor, and the server resolves the current bridge session
  on every tick from the MobMachine's `member_session_bindings` map.
  Respawn atomically rotates the bridge session via the
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: Some }` effect; the
  channel survives without any SDK round-trip. A terminal
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: None }` closes the
  channel with `RealtimeErrorCode::BindingReleased`.

RealtimeChannelConfig.description:
Per-channel runtime knobs negotiated at open time.

Additive fields only — clients that do not carry this struct inherit the
server-default behavior via `#[serde(default)]` on the parent
[`RealtimeOpenRequest`].

tool_timeout_ms.description:
Maximum wall-clock time a tool dispatch may take before the channel
gives up and injects a synthetic tool-error result. `None` disables the
deadline (product explicitly opted into unlimited tools); omitting the
field leaves the server default in place.

RealtimeChannelRole.description: Opening role for a realtime channel.

RealtimeChannelTarget.description:
Target for a public realtime channel.

Two variants, one for each addressing mode:

- `SessionTarget` — standalone sessions (no mob-member continuity). The
  session id is pinned for the channel's lifetime; when that session
  ends, the channel ends.

- `MobMember` — mob-member continuity (W3-H / dogma #4). Identity is the
  canonical anchor, and the server resolves the current bridge session
  on every tick from the MobMachine's `member_session_bindings` map.
  Respawn atomically rotates the bridge session via the
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: Some }` effect; the
  channel survives without any SDK round-trip. A terminal
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: None }` closes the
  channel with `RealtimeErrorCode::BindingReleased`.

RealtimeReconnectPolicy.description: Public reconnect policy for a realtime channel.

RealtimeTurningMode.description: Turning mode for a realtime channel.

channel_config.description:
Optional per-channel runtime knobs (tool budget, etc.). Omitted means
"use server defaults".

RealtimeChannelTarget.description:
Target for a public realtime channel.

Two variants, one for each addressing mode:

- `SessionTarget` — standalone sessions (no mob-member continuity). The
  session id is pinned for the channel's lifetime; when that session
  ends, the channel ends.

- `MobMember` — mob-member continuity (W3-H / dogma #4). Identity is the
  canonical anchor, and the server resolves the current bridge session
  on every tick from the MobMachine's `member_session_bindings` map.
  Respawn atomically rotates the bridge session via the

  channel survives without any SDK round-trip. A terminal
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: None }` closes the
  channel with `RealtimeErrorCode::BindingReleased`.

RuntimeAcceptParams.description:
Request payload for `runtime/session_submit`.

The runtime input body is owned by `meerkat-runtime`; contracts cannot
depend on the runtime crate without inverting the dependency graph. The
envelope is canonical here and the runtime crate remains the typed
authority over the `input` discriminator and variant body.
RuntimeAcceptParams.title: RuntimeAcceptParams

SkillKey.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a skill.

This is the single identity carried across every surface — the wire parses
directly into this struct, tools receive this struct, the registry stores
this struct. There is no slash-delimited string path form.

SkillRef.description:
Structured skill reference for wire/surface input.

Post-V4 this has exactly one variant — `Structured` — and is kept only as a
typed enum to leave room for future source-scoped variants (e.g. alias or
capability-scoped forms) without changing the trait surface. No legacy
string form, no slash-delimited path, no untagged fallback.

SkillsParams.description:
Skills parameters for session/turn requests.

`preload_skills` and `skill_refs` both carry typed `SkillKey`s — there is
no legacy string path. Wire ingress parses JSON objects directly to
`SkillKey` (`{"source_uuid":"…","skill_name":"…"}`); malformed input is a
typed ingress error, not a legacy-upgrade.

`Some([])` is normalized to `None` to prevent silent misconfiguration.
SkillsParams.title: SkillsParams

BlobId.description:
Canonical realm-local blob identifier.

The identifier is content-addressed, but storage and GC semantics remain
realm-scoped.

oneOf.description: Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.
Durable blob reference used by persisted history/runtime state.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.

ContentInput.description:
Input content that can be either a plain text string or multimodal content blocks.

Deserializes from either `"text"` or `[{type: "text", text: "..."}, ...]`.
Provides `From<String>` and `From<&str>` for ergonomic construction.

role_name.description: Role name (profile key) for the helper in the mob roster.

oneOf.description:
Skip materially late pending occurrences instead of catching them up.

The scheduler applies a small internal grace window so normal tick
jitter does not immediately misfire on-time work.

Catch up overdue pending occurrences only while they remain within the
configured lateness window.

OutputSchema.description:
Configuration for structured output extraction.

When provided to an agent, the agent will perform an extraction turn after
completing the agentic work, forcing the LLM to output validated JSON that
conforms to the provided schema. The extraction JSON becomes the final
response text (schema-only) in [`RunResult`].

compat.description: Compatibility mode for provider lowering (lossy or strict)

name.description: Optional name for the schema (used by some providers)

schema.description: The JSON schema that the output must conform to

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict schema validation (provider-specific)

PeerMeta.description:
Supplementary metadata attached to a peer for discovery.

The peer's routing name lives in `TrustedPeer.name` / `comms_name`.
`PeerMeta` carries additional context: what the agent does and
arbitrary key/value labels.

# Serde

Both fields use `skip_serializing_if`, so `PeerMeta::default()`
serializes to `{}`.

description.description: What this agent does (e.g. "Reviews pull requests for style issues").

labels.description:
Arbitrary key/value labels (e.g. `{"lang": "rust", "team": "infra"}`).

`BTreeMap` keeps keys sorted for deterministic serialization.

RenderClass.description:
Standardized rendering class for injected ordinary work.

This metadata is for normalization/injection rendering, not for core
runtime scheduling. It lets the system consistently frame peer messages,
external events, tool-scope notices, and similar inputs when they are
rendered into model-visible context.

RenderMetadata.description: Optional rendering metadata carried alongside ordinary work content.

RenderSalience.description:
Constrained salience metadata for injected ordinary work.

This is intentionally a small closed enum so call sites do not invent
arbitrary free-text urgency language.

SchemaFormat.description: Schema format versions supported by Meerkat.

SessionId.description: Unique identifier for a session (UUID v7 for time-ordering)

SkillKey.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a skill.

This is the single identity carried across every surface — the wire parses
directly into this struct, tools receive this struct, the registry stores
this struct. There is no slash-delimited string path form.

SkillRef.description:
Structured skill reference for wire/surface input.

Post-V4 this has exactly one variant — `Structured` — and is kept only as a
typed enum to leave room for future source-scoped variants (e.g. alias or
capability-scoped forms) without changing the trait surface. No legacy
string form, no slash-delimited path, no untagged fallback.

ToolAccessPolicy.description: Tool access control for delegated branches

ToolName.description:
Canonical typed name for an LLM-callable tool.

The model-facing wire shape is still the provider's string tool name, but
internal policy, routing, and registry surfaces carry this handle so raw
strings do not become the tool identity owner.
